# Aira — Magical Action Reels · Master Set (Heavy-Detail VEO 3.1 / Nano Banana Pro)

<aside>
✨

A from-scratch master set of 20 fully-detailed Aira action reel concepts, each engineered as ONE seamless animated reel — every frame chains invisibly into the next (last frame = next frame's first), built around one impossible "magical" move per concept with locked transform keyframes, a perfect-loop close, drone + ground + FPV camera language, dual audio (per-frame diegetic + a separate Master Track Brief), and fully timecoded action. Vivid saturated grade, real motion blur, distinct still-vs-video angles. 9:16 throughout. Stills → Nano Banana Pro · Motion → VEO 3.1.

</aside>

## 🎛️ Global Master Spec — read once, applies to all 20

SUBJECT LOCK: Aira is a self-aware adult performer with fluid, human body mechanics and real micro-expressions for every action; identity locked to the reference sheet, zero drift, never sexualized. Paste the Aira face-lock into every `IDENTITY LOCK` placeholder.

FORMAT: 9:16 vertical · stills generated in Nano Banana Pro · motion in VEO 3.1 · 24fps base with speed-ramps called per frame.

GRADE LOCK: vivid, saturated, alive color — deep rich blacks, clean highlights, full mid-tone, tasteful motion blur; never dull, washed, or flat.

AUDIO LOCK (dual-block): every VIDEO prompt carries an in-prompt AUDIO block of timed diegetic sound (engine, footfall, cloth, breath, impact) and no subject speaking voice — narrator VO only where marked. Each concept also ends with a separate MASTER TRACK BRIEF for the background score (kept low under the diegetic SFX, never a full mix).

CONTINUITY LOCK: frames are chained 6–8 per concept; each frame ends on an exit pose/cue that is the next frame's entry, so joining all frames plays as one continuous animated shot. The final frame resolves back to Frame 1 for a seamless loop.

TWIST LOCK: every concept has exactly ONE impossible move (the "magic"), bracketed by a locked START keyframe and END keyframe so the transform interpolates cleanly without inventing a new face, body, or vehicle.

### ➕ Enhanced per-frame template (extra sections beyond the reference)

Each frame is written as a separate IMAGE PROMPT and VIDEO PROMPT.

- IMAGE adds: `FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES`, `BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT`, `PHYSICS (what is animated)`, `CAMERA ANGLE (this frame's distinct angle — drone / low / FPV / crane / eye-macro)`, `FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF` — on top of Identity Lock, Wardrobe, Hair, Makeup, Hands & Nails, Facial Micro-Dynamics, 8-layer Kinetic Stillness, Spatial Logic, Lighting, Framing, Lens+DOF+Bokeh, Composition Ref, Atmosphere, Color Grade, Texture & Micro-Detail, Negative.
- VIDEO adds: `DRONE / RIG NOTE`, `SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE` (beats to ~0.2–0.5s), `PHYSICS & MOTION`, `SPEED RAMP`, `FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF`, plus the dual AUDIO block — on top of Shot Type, Camera Movement, Lens, Lighting, Environment, Duration, Frame-Rate+Motion-Blur, Lip-Sync, Negative.

## 🌀 CONCEPT 01 — TUNNEL LAUNCH · Light-Warp

Pillar / Format: Pillar 1 — Kinetic Hero (night hypercar launch, one warp-speed impossible move).

Hero: Aira at the wheel of a matte-graphite mid-engine hypercar, single accent of a hot-orange brake-caliper glow.

Setting: a neon-ribbed city underpass at night — wet black asphalt mirroring magenta-and-cyan light strips, sodium pools, faint exhaust haze drifting in the cold air (rich saturated night lane).

Wardrobe (AW): a fitted matte-black technical racing jacket with a subtle graphite weave and a high modest collar, slim moto trousers, full-coverage; nothing loose to snag.

Hair (AP): a sleek low racing braid with a clean center part and lacquered flyaway control, a few face-framing strands free to react to motion, color locked to reference.

Makeup (AQ): clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft bronze contour, a defined brow, a deep berry stained lip, crisp inner-corner brighten; real pores and vellus texture.

Footwear: low-profile black driving shoes with a thin grippy sole and a single graphite lace-flash, laces tucked.

Accessories: slim matte-black driving gloves, a low-profile titanium ear-cuff, no dangling elements.

The Impossible Move (one magic): at launch the tunnel and car smear into ribbons of warp-speed light — reality stretches to streaking neon filaments around a still-recognizable Aira, then snaps back to a clean tunnel sprint. START keyframe = Frame 3 (launch, lights begin to streak); END keyframe = Frame 5 (warp resolves, car mid-tunnel).

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "Some doors only open at full throttle."

Duration: 26 seconds · 7 frames.

Mood: ELECTRIC, controlled, predatory-calm.

Composition signature: centered hero with deep one-point tunnel perspective funnelling to a vanishing point of light.

Camera Language: opens on a descending drone, hands to a low ground-tracking rig, into an in-car gimbal, an FPV chase through the warp, a crane rise on resolve, an eye-macro for the line, and a drone pull-back to loop.

Grade / Color Script: saturated neon-noir — magenta + cyan key, hot-orange caliper accent, deep uncrushed blacks, wet specular highlights, full mid-tone, zero murk.

Narrator VO (AR): lands Frame 6 — "Some doors only open at full throttle." (English, warm natural Indian accent; Aira does not speak on camera.)

Frame-Join Continuity: each frame exits on the exact pose/lighting state that opens the next; Frame 7 resolves to the Frame 1 tunnel-mouth hook for a seamless loop.

Identity safety: Aira is an adult; wardrobe fully modest; tone is high-performance and cinematic, never sexualized.

---

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

WARDROBE: the fitted matte-black racing jacket with graphite weave and high modest collar, one cold magenta rim sliding along the shoulder seam, pristine, zero lint or snags.

HAIR: the sleek low racing braid, lacquered control with a few face-framing strands catching the cyan strip, razor-clean hairline, individual strands real, color locked.

MAKEUP: clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft bronze contour, defined brow, deep berry stained lip with one wet light-point, inner-corner brighten; real pores.

FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES: low black driving shoes with graphite lace-flash; slim matte gloves; titanium ear-cuff catching a tiny cyan glint.

HANDS & NAILS: right hand resting on the cold door edge, fingertips light with natural nail-bed color, no white-knuckle tension; left hand relaxed at her thigh; short clean almond nails in a graphite-nude gloss.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: chin level, a calm predatory quarter-smile pulling the left corner a hair tighter, lips parted barely 1mm on a held breath, eyes direct to lens with a crisp twin neon catchlight, the faintest focused tension at the outer brow.

BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT: standing at the open driver door, torso rotated about 8° to open the right shoulder to lens, weight settled on the back leg with a 3° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long and ready (living stillness).

PHYSICS (what is animated): drifting exhaust haze, a slow flicker in the neon strips, a faint wet shimmer where light meets the asphalt — environment alive, subject poised.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a free strand drifting at her temple in the cold air; (2) exhaust haze curling low past the sill; (3) a neon strip flickering soft on the wall; (4) a reflection rippling in a thin water sheet on the asphalt; (5) chest rising on a slow shallow inhale; (6) a magenta specular sliding a few mm along the wet hood; (7) the jacket weave catching a micro-shift of rim-light as she breathes; (8) a slow eyelash settle completing a blink.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the wet asphalt and the car's front wing nearest lens at ~20% depth, tack sharp. MIDGROUND — Aira at the open door, the hero. BACKGROUND — the tunnel mouth funnelling to a soft neon vanishing point, clean separation, no clutter.

CAMERA ANGLE: a slow drone descent from high-front easing down to a clean chest-up hero level — establishing the tunnel scale before settling on her.

LIGHTING: KEY — a cool magenta wall-strip keying from camera-left. FILL — a soft cyan bounce from the wet road lifting the under-jaw clean. RIM — a hot-orange caliper-glow kiss separating her shoulder from the dark. PRACTICAL — sodium pools and neon strips. SEPARATION — the tunnel pulled deep so she reads ~2 stops up. BOUNCE — a wet-road kick adding a glossy catch to the jacket.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium, chest-up, 12% headroom, the car wing entering the lower third, rich negative space camera-left to receive the launch.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium framing holds Aira at about 12% headroom, chest-up, the car wing entering the lower third, rich negative space camera-left to receive the launch. She is standing at the open driver door, torso rotated about 8° to open the right shoulder to lens, weight settled on the back leg with a 3° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long and ready. The camera holds a mid distance, Aira the sharp central figure of the Tunnel Launch beat, with the rich negative space opening camera-left. She faces front to the lens. Her pose reads as standing at the open driver door, torso rotated about 8° to open the right shoulder to lens, weight settled on the back leg with a 3° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long and ready (living stillness) — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm, f/2.0; focus split between her near eye and the door edge; the tunnel melting to creamy neon bokeh; soft circular blooms off the wet highlights; gentle highlight roll-off, no clipping.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: a neon-noir automotive hero still — driver-at-door, deep tunnel perspective, scroll-stopping.

ATMOSPHERE: electric, cold, charged — the hush of a held breath before ignition.

COLOR GRADE: saturated neon-noir — magenta + cyan with a hot-orange accent, deep uncrushed blacks, wet speculars, full mid-tone, zero grain, no murk.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: the matte hood reads as real micro-flake paint with crisp neon refractions; the wet asphalt shows true puddle reflections; jacket weave reads thread-real; soft peach-fuzz catches the rim at her jaw; haze drifts through the strip-light.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: ends locked on her hand on the door and the calm direct gaze — Frame 2 opens on that exact hand already pulling the door and sliding into the seat.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes (unintentional), garbled text/badges, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, plastic or over-smoothed skin, oversharpened, AI artifacts, watermarks, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts, cross-eyed, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin stiffness, dead eyes, crushed muddy blacks, garish oversaturation, flat dull light.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: establishing-to-hero, chest-up resolve; subject fills ~45% rising to ~55% of the vertical frame.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: cinema drone descent (high-front → eye level), one smooth controlled move, no jitter.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the drone eases down and settles to a near-locked hero frame with a faint 4% push-in.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.0, creamy neon fall-off.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE: 0.0–2.0s drone descends, tunnel scale revealed, Aira still at the door; 2.0–4.4s she lifts her gaze to lens as the calm quarter-smile blooms on a soft inhale; 4.4–6.0s her hand settles on the door edge, the held-breath beat before ignition.

PHYSICS & MOTION: real drifting haze, genuine wet reflections shifting with the drone parallax, natural strand movement — no teleporting, no pop-in.

LIGHTING: cool magenta key with cyan road-bounce; hot-orange caliper rim steady; neon strips live; tunnel held deep.

ENVIRONMENT: neon underpass at night, wet asphalt, drifting exhaust haze, flickering strips.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time throughout.

AUDIO: diegetic only — ambient: a low cold room-tone and a faint electrical neon hum throughout; SFX: a soft distant city rumble, a gentle metallic door-handle tick at 4.6s (short tail), a faint water-drip at 2.8s; reaction: a soft audible breath at 2.2s. No subject voice. The master track is just kindling — a low sub-drone under the room-tone (present, never full). VO — none this frame.

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:00–0:03 of 26s).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural motion blur on the drone move; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips composed.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: last beat = hand on door + direct gaze; Frame 2 begins on that hand pulling the door open.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, audio desync, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker.

### Frame 2 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

WARDROBE: the matte-black racing jacket, continuity-locked, the collar catching a cyan rim as she settles into the seat, fully modest, pristine.

HAIR: the sleek low braid, continuity-locked, one strand drifting as she ducks in, lacquered sheen, color locked.

MAKEUP: clean kinetic glow, continuity-locked, berry lip with a wet point, real pores.

FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES: the driving shoe pressing the sill then the pedal floor; gloves flexing as she grips; ear-cuff glinting cyan.

HANDS & NAILS: right hand closing on the alcantara wheel at 9 o'clock, left hand settling on the metal shifter, fingers naturally curved, natural nail-bed color, no white-knuckle tension.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: eyes narrowing a hair into focus, lips pressing on a slow exhale, a micro-tightening at the jaw — the readiness beat before launch.

BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT: seated, spine braced to the bucket seat, shoulders squared, chin level, weight grounded through the hips, forearms loaded but loose.

PHYSICS (what is animated): dash telltales pulsing, a faint rev-shudder in the cabin, haze visible through the windshield, her braid settling from the duck-in.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a strand settling at her temple; (2) dash telltales pulsing warm; (3) a faint cabin vibration as revs build; (4) neon reflections sliding across the curved windshield; (5) chest on a slow controlled exhale; (6) a specular crawling along the wheel rim; (7) glove leather flexing micro as she grips; (8) a slow blink locking focus.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the wheel rim and shifter nearest lens at ~18% depth, tack sharp. MIDGROUND — Aira's face and braced shoulders, the hero. BACKGROUND — the neon tunnel through the windshield, softly defocused.

CAMERA ANGLE: an in-car gimbal from the dash looking back — intimate three-quarter on her face with the tunnel reading through the glass.

LIGHTING: KEY — magenta tunnel light through the windshield. FILL — soft dash-glow lifting the under-jaw. RIM — cyan strip catching the braid. PRACTICAL — dash telltales. SEPARATION — the dark cabin pulling her forward. BOUNCE — a soft wheel-glint kick.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium close, 11% headroom, wheel and shifter in the lower third, negative space camera-right.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium close framing holds Aira at about 11% headroom, wheel and shifter in the lower third, negative space camera-right. She is seated, spine braced to the bucket seat, shoulders squared, chin level, weight grounded through the hips, forearms loaded but loose. The camera holds an intimate distance, Aira the sharp central figure of the Tunnel Launch beat, with the rich negative space opening camera-right. She faces front to the lens. Her pose reads as seated, spine braced to the bucket seat, shoulders squared, chin level, weight grounded through the hips, forearms loaded but loose — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 40mm, f/2.0; focus split between her near eye and the shifter; the windshield neon melting to creamy bokeh; soft blooms off the telltales; gentle roll-off.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: an intimate cockpit readiness still — driver braced, neon beyond glass, charged.

ATMOSPHERE: focused, coiled, electric — the cabin humming before release.

COLOR GRADE: saturated neon-noir, magenta-cyan with warm dash accents, deep blacks, full mid-tone, no murk.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: alcantara wheel reads as real suede grain, glove leather creases true, windshield shows real neon streaks, skin micro-texture intact under the dash glow.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: opens on the hand from Frame 1 pulling the door; ends on hands loaded at wheel + shifter and a slow blink — Frame 3 opens on those same hands snapping into the launch.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes, garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, plastic skin, over-smoothed, oversharpened, AI artifacts, watermarks, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts, cross-eyed, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin stiffness, dead eyes, crushed blacks, garish oversaturation, flat light.

### Frame 2 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: intimate cockpit medium-close; subject fills ~58%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: in-car gimbal on a dash mount, micro-stabilized; no drone this frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a slow 3% push from the dash with a faint 2° tilt up to her eyes, one flowing move.

LENS: 40mm, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE: 0.0–0.9s her hand (from Frame 1) pulls the door shut, cabin seals; 0.9–2.4s she grips the wheel at 9 o'clock and sets her left on the shifter; 2.4–3.9s eyes narrow into focus on a slow exhale; 3.9–5.1s a micro-jaw set, revs building, the readiness peak.

PHYSICS & MOTION: real cabin micro-shudder as revs climb, true reflections sliding on the glass with the push, natural braid settle — grounded physics.

LIGHTING: magenta through-glass key, warm dash fill, cyan braid rim; cabin held dark.

ENVIRONMENT: hypercar cockpit, neon tunnel beyond the windshield, drifting haze.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time.

AUDIO: diegetic only — ambient: muffled cabin tone, a low building engine spool throughout; SFX: a solid door-seal thunk at 0.9s, a leather-glove creak at 1.5s, a metallic shifter-set click at 2.1s, a rising turbo whine 2.4–5.1s; reaction: a slow controlled exhale at 2.7s. No subject voice. Master track adds a low pulsing sub under the spool (present, never full). VO — none.

DURATION: 4 seconds (0:03–0:07 of 26s).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, gentle natural blur; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: ends on loaded hands + revs peaking; Frame 3 opens on the same hands snapping the launch as the lights begin to streak.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, audio desync, wardrobe drift, camera shake, physics violation, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker.

### Frame 3 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. TRANSFORM KEYFRAME NOTE: this is the locked START keyframe of the light-warp move — face fully recognizable, cabin and tunnel intact, the neon strips just beginning to elongate into the first light-streaks at the frame edges. Lock this START so Frame 4's warp interpolates cleanly to the full-warp peak and resolves at the Frame 5 END.

WARDROBE: the matte-black jacket pressed back into the seat by launch g-force, collar taut, fully modest, pristine.

HAIR: the braid pressed back, face-framing strands streaming straight back in the acceleration, color locked under the sheen.

MAKEUP: clean kinetic glow holding, berry lip, real pores, a cool neon wash rising on the cheekbone.

FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES: driving shoe flat to the floor on the throttle, glove knuckles tightening, ear-cuff streaking a tiny cyan trail.

HANDS & NAILS: both hands firm on the wheel as it loads, forearms tensioning naturally, natural nail-bed color, controlled grip — strong, not white-knuckled.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: eyes wide and alive with focused exhilaration, lips parted on the g-force, a thrilled micro-flare at the nostril, brow set — pure launch adrenaline, identity clear.

BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT: body pressed into the bucket, head braced to the rest, shoulders pinned by acceleration, core engaged — true g-load physics.

PHYSICS (what is animated): neon strips elongating to first streaks, the windshield smearing light, haze ripping past, the cabin shuddering at launch.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) strands streaming straight back; (2) neon strips beginning to elongate; (3) the windshield smearing first light-trails; (4) haze tearing past the glass; (5) chest pressed on a held adrenaline breath; (6) speculars stretching along the wheel and dash; (7) glove leather taut over knuckles; (8) pupils sharp and locked forward.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the loading wheel nearest lens, sharp. MIDGROUND — Aira pinned at launch, the hero. BACKGROUND — the tunnel starting to stretch into light at the edges.

CAMERA ANGLE: a low ground-tracking rig rushing alongside the launching car, tilting up to catch her through the side glass — speed read from the ground.

LIGHTING: KEY — magenta now streaking. FILL — cyan road-bounce stretching. RIM — hot-orange caliper flaring with speed. PRACTICAL — strips elongating. SEPARATION — the stretching tunnel. BOUNCE — multiplying speculars on the smearing glass.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium, 10% headroom, wheel in the lower third, edges opening to first streaks.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium framing holds Aira at about 10% headroom, wheel in the lower third, edges opening to first streaks. She is body pressed into the bucket, head braced to the rest, shoulders pinned by acceleration, core engaged — true g-load physics. The camera holds a mid distance, Aira the sharp central figure of the Tunnel Launch beat, centred in frame. She faces front to the lens. Her pose reads as body pressed into the bucket, head braced to the rest, shoulders pinned by acceleration, core engaged — true g-load physics — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm, f/2.0; focus on her near eye as the background stretches; first light-trails rendering as soft neon streaks; gentle roll-off.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: the launch-ignition still — driver pinned, tunnel beginning to warp, kinetic.

ATMOSPHERE: explosive, exhilarated, charged — the instant the world starts to stretch.

### Frame 3 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

### Frame 7 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: loop-close establishing; subject ~50% as the drone rises.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: cinema drone pull-back (hero level → high-front), the mirror of Frame 1's descent.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the drone rises and pulls back to the Frame 1 establishing position; one smooth move, no jitter.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE: 0.0–1.8s she settles back at the open door, hand on the edge, the composed quarter-smile returning; 1.8–3.9s a held breath as the drone rises; 3.9–6.0s the framing matches Frame 1 exactly, ready to loop.

PHYSICS & MOTION: real drifting haze and wet reflections shifting with the drone parallax, natural strand movement — matched to Frame 1.

LIGHTING: cool magenta key, cyan road-bounce, hot-orange caliper rim — matched to Frame 1.

ENVIRONMENT: the neon underpass at night, wet asphalt, drifting haze.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time.

AUDIO: diegetic only — ambient: low cold room-tone and faint neon hum; SFX: a soft door-handle tick at 2.1s, a distant city rumble, a faint water-drip at 3.9s; reaction: a soft breath at 1.8s. No subject voice. Master track resolves on a sustained warm chord so the reel loops with no audible seam (present, never full). VO — none.

DURATION: 4 seconds (0:22–0:26 of 26s).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone rise; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips composed.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: resolves to the exact Frame 1 hook (hand on door, direct gaze, drone high-front) so Frame 7 → Frame 1 loops invisibly.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, audio desync, wardrobe drift, camera shake, physics violation, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker, visible loop seam.

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 01 — full reel, anchored at the Frame 4 light-warp and the Frame 6 VO): an electric neon-noir score, glossy and propulsive — a deep pulsing sub-bass night-groove under a glowing analog arpeggio, a tight gated-snare backbeat entering at launch, and a neon-bright lead motif tracing the light-ribbons. Tempo driving around 104–110 BPM, key minor and moody with a bright neon top. The bed kindles low under the door-beat (Frames 1–2), surges on the launch (Frame 3), swells to its electric peak on the light-warp (Frame 4), eases through the triumphant resolve (Frame 5), ducks cleanly under the narrator line "Some doors only open at full throttle." (Frame 6) so the VO sits forward, and resolves on a glowing sustained synth-chord at Frame 7 so the reel loops seamlessly with no audible seam. Keep it under the diegetic engine, tyre, wind and door SFX — electric and present, never a full driving mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

## 💨 CONCEPT 02 — VIOLENT DRIFT · Galaxy Ember-Dust

Pillar / Format: Pillar 1 — Kinetic Hero (dusk drift, one cosmic smoke-transform; silent reel, music + diegetic only).

Hero: Aira sliding a wide-body matte-crimson drift coupe, single accent of glowing cyan underglow.

Setting: a vast empty dockyard at golden-hour dusk — cracked concrete, stacked rust-orange shipping containers, low sun flaring between gaps, sea-haze drifting (warm-saturated dusk lane).

Wardrobe (AW): a fitted charcoal racing bomber with crimson piping and a high modest collar, slim technical trousers, full-coverage, nothing loose.

Hair (AP): a high sleek ponytail with a clean part and lacquered control, the tail free to whip with motion, color locked to reference.

Makeup (AQ): clean kinetic glow — warm satin skin, soft bronze contour, defined brow, a brick-rose lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores.

Footwear: crimson-soled black driving shoes, laces tucked.

Accessories: fingerless carbon driving gloves, a thin matte choker-mic prop (non-speaking), no dangles.

The Impossible Move (one magic): mid-drift the billowing tire-smoke ignites into a swirling galaxy of ember-dust — embers, stardust and nebula-cloud spiralling off the rear arches around a still-recognizable Aira, then settling back to ordinary smoke. START = Frame 3 (full drift, smoke billowing); END = Frame 5 (galaxy settling, car straightening).

Concept Line / Twist payoff: (silent — no spoken or narrated line; the cosmic smoke IS the payoff.)

Duration: 24 seconds · 6 frames.

Mood: FIERCE, molten, cosmic-calm.

Composition signature: low wide hero with the drift arc sweeping smoke across clean negative space.

Camera Language: opens on a drone orbit, hands to a low chase rig, an in-car gimbal at the initiation, an FPV swing through the galaxy-smoke, a high crane on settle, and a drone pull-up to loop.

Grade / Color Script: saturated warm-dusk — golden sun flare, rust-orange + crimson, cyan underglow accent, deep blacks, full mid-tone, the galaxy beat shimmering violet-magenta starlight.

Voice: SILENT — no subject voice, no narrator; diegetic SFX + master track only.

Frame-Join Continuity: each frame exits on the exact pose/smoke-state that opens the next; Frame 6 resolves to the Frame 1 dockyard hook for a seamless loop.

Identity safety: Aira is an adult; wardrobe fully modest; tone is high-performance and cinematic, never sexualized.

### Frame 3 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: low chase medium; subject + smoke fill ~60%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: low chase rig (speed-arm) swinging behind the rear arch; ultra-stable at speed.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the rig swings wide and tracks the drift arc with a faint 3° tilt up through the smoke; one driving move.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.2.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE: 0.0–0.6s the rear breaks fully loose, smoke explodes off the arches; 0.6–1.8s she catches it on opposite-lock, ponytail streaming, eyes alive; 1.8–3.3s smoke billows dense, first embers kindle at the edges; 3.3–4.5s the cloud brightens with star-glints, cueing the bloom.

PHYSICS & MOTION: authentic lateral-g on body and hair, real smoke volume rolling, genuine ember-kindle building — no cheating.

LIGHTING: gold sun through smoke, amber bounce, cyan underglow firing the smoke-base.

ENVIRONMENT: the dockyard sweeping past a wall of dense smoke beginning to spark.

SPEED RAMP: 100% to a slight 90% ease entering the bloom.

AUDIO: diegetic-led — ambient: hard tyre-scrub roar; SFX: a V8 throttle-bark crescendo 0.0–1.5s, a continuous tyre-scream 0.6–4.5s, a low ember-crackle kindling 1.8–4.5s, a wind-roar bed; reaction: a sharp adrenaline inhale at 0.8s. No subject voice, no VO. Master track surges a driving pulse under the roar (present, never full).

DURATION: 4 seconds (0:08–0:12 of 24s).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps with heavy natural motion blur on the smoke, 180° shutter; a touch of slow-mo easing in.

LIP-SYNC: none.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: ends on star-glints kindling at peak smoke; Frame 4 opens directly into the full galaxy bloom from this state.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping (unintended), identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, audio desync, wardrobe drift, unmotivated shake, physics violation, inventing a new face in the smoke, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: identical hook to Frame 1 — driver-at-door, container backdrop, engineered to loop invisibly.

ATMOSPHERE: fierce, warm, hushed — the run resolving softly back into the opening breath where it began.

COLOR GRADE: saturated warm-dusk — golden flare, rust-orange + crimson, cyan accent, deep blacks, full mid-tone, no murk — matched to Frame 1.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: crimson micro-flake paint, concrete grit, thread-real bomber fabric, soft peach-fuzz at the jaw, drifting haze — all matched to Frame 1 for a clean loop.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: matches Frame 1 exactly — hand on roof, calm direct gaze — so the reel loops back to Frame 1 with no visible seam.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes, garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, plastic skin, over-smoothed, oversharpened, AI artifacts, watermarks, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts, cross-eyed, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin stiffness, dead eyes, crushed blacks, garish oversaturation, flat light.

### Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: loop-close establishing; subject ~50% as the drone rises.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: cinema drone pull-up (low hero → high quarter-orbit), the mirror of Frame 1's orbit-descent.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the drone rises and arcs back to the Frame 1 establishing position; one smooth move, no jitter.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.2.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE: 0.0–1.8s she settles back at the door, hand flat on the roof, the composed quarter-smile returning; 1.8–3.9s a held breath as the drone rises; 3.9–6.0s the framing matches Frame 1 exactly, ready to loop.

PHYSICS & MOTION: real drifting haze and heat-shimmer shifting with the drone parallax, natural ponytail sway — matched to Frame 1.

LIGHTING: warm sun key, amber concrete-bounce, cyan underglow rim — matched to Frame 1.

ENVIRONMENT: the dockyard at dusk, containers, drifting sea-haze.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time.

AUDIO: diegetic only — ambient: warm dock room-tone, distant gulls, faint sea-breeze; SFX: a soft roof-tap at 2.1s, distant rigging-clink, a faint idle burble at 3.9s; reaction: a soft breath at 1.8s. No subject voice, no VO. Master track resolves on a sustained warm chord so the reel loops with no audible seam (present, never full).

DURATION: 4 seconds (0:20–0:24 of 24s).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone rise; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips composed.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: resolves to the exact Frame 1 hook (hand on roof, direct gaze, drone high-side) so Frame 6 → Frame 1 loops invisibly.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, audio desync, wardrobe drift, camera shake, physics violation, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker, visible loop seam.

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 02 — full reel, fully instrumental, anchored at the Frame 4 galaxy bloom): a fierce cosmic-cinematic bed, molten and weightless — a deep pulsing sub-bass under a wide analog brass-synth swell and a tight tom-driven groove that enters at the initiation, with a shimmering granular star-pad tracing the ember-dust. Tempo driving around 96–102 BPM, key minor turning luminous. The bed kindles low under the door-beat (Frame 1), tightens on the initiation (Frame 2), surges with the full drift (Frame 3), swells to its cosmic peak on the galaxy ember-dust bloom (Frame 4), eases through the triumphant straighten (Frame 5), and resolves on a sustained warm chord at Frame 6 so the reel loops seamlessly with no audible seam. Keep it under the diegetic engine, tyre-scream, ember-crackle and wind SFX — fierce and present, never a full driving mix, and with no vocals of any kind (silent reel). (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

## ↩️ CONCEPT 03 — REVERSE U-TURN · Time-Rewind Ripple

Pillar / Format: Pillar 1 — Kinetic Hero (dawn handbrake 180, one time-rewind impossible move; narrated reel).

Hero: Aira flat-spinning a pearl-white rally sedan with brushed-gold accents, single accent of amber fog-lamp glow.

Setting: a misty mountain hairpin overlook at first light — wet dark tarmac, pine forest dropping into a valley of low fog, gold dawn cracking the ridgeline, dew hanging in the air (cool-to-warm saturated dawn lane).

Wardrobe (AW): a fitted slate rally jacket with gold zip-piping and a high modest collar, slim technical trousers, full-coverage, nothing loose.

Hair (AP): a sleek low twin-braid pulled tight with lacquered control, a few face-framing strands free, color locked to reference.

Makeup (AQ): clean kinetic glow — cool-neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a muted rosewood lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores.

Footwear: grippy slate driving shoes with a gold heel-flash, laces tucked.

Accessories: thin slate driving gloves, a small gold collar-pin, no dangles.

The Impossible Move (one magic): at the apex of the 180 a time-rewind ripple sweeps outward from the car — dew, dust, fallen pine-needles and a spray of water visibly reverse and un-fall, the world briefly rewinding in a glassy shockwave while Aira stays forward and present, then time snaps to normal. START = Frame 3 (apex, ripple begins); END = Frame 5 (ripple resolves, car pointed back the way it came).

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "Sometimes you go back to go forward."

Duration: 24 seconds · 6 frames.

Mood: POISED, mystical, precise.

Composition signature: centered hero on the hairpin with the valley of fog as deep negative space.

Camera Language: opens on a top-down drone spin, hands to a low chase rig, an in-car gimbal at the initiation, an FPV orbit through the rewind ripple, a crane rise on resolve, and a drone lift to loop.

Grade / Color Script: saturated dawn — cool teal fog shadows, warm gold dawn key, pearl-white + brushed-gold accents, deep blacks, full mid-tone; the rewind beat shimmering with a glassy chrome-prism edge.

Narrator VO (AR): lands Frame 5 — "Sometimes you go back to go forward." (English, warm natural Indian accent; Aira does not speak on camera.)

Frame-Join Continuity: each frame exits on the exact pose/ripple-state that opens the next; Frame 6 resolves to the Frame 1 hairpin hook for a seamless loop.

Identity safety: Aira is an adult; wardrobe fully modest; tone is precise and cinematic, never sexualized.

---

ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: loop-close establishing; subject ~50% as the drone lifts.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: cinema drone lift (low hero → high overhead), the mirror of Frame 1's descent.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the drone rises and pulls back to the Frame 1 overhead establishing position; one smooth move, no jitter.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.2.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE: 0.0–1.8s she settles back at the door, hand flat on the roof, the composed quarter-smile returning; 1.8–3.9s a held breath as the drone lifts; 3.9–6.0s the framing matches Frame 1 exactly, ready to loop.

PHYSICS & MOTION: real curling fog and drifting dew shifting with the drone parallax, natural strand movement — matched to Frame 1.

LIGHTING: warm gold key, teal fog-bounce, amber fog-lamp rim — matched to Frame 1.

ENVIRONMENT: the mountain hairpin at first light, pines, low valley fog.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time.

AUDIO: diegetic only — ambient: cool dawn room-tone, distant birdsong, faint mountain breeze; SFX: a soft roof-tap at 2.1s, a distant valley echo, a faint dew-drip at 3.9s; reaction: a soft breath at 1.8s. No subject voice. Master track resolves on a sustained warm chord so the reel loops with no audible seam (present, never full). VO — none.

DURATION: 4 seconds (0:20–0:24 of 24s).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone lift; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips composed.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: resolves to the exact Frame 1 hook (hand on roof, direct gaze, drone overhead) so Frame 6 → Frame 1 loops invisibly.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, audio desync, wardrobe drift, camera shake, physics violation, mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker, visible loop seam.

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 03 — full reel, anchored at the Frame 4 time-rewind and the Frame 5 VO): a poised mystical-cinematic score, glassy and precise — a soft reversed-piano motif and a glassy bowed-glass pad over a deep sub-pulse, with a delicate crystalline arpeggio tracing the ripple and a slow timpani heartbeat felt under it. Tempo measured around 90–96 BPM, key minor turning luminous. The bed kindles low under the door-beat (Frame 1), tightens on the initiation (Frame 2), surges with the apex spin (Frame 3), swells to its glassy peak on the time-rewind ripple (Frame 4, with a tasteful reversed-reverb swell), ducks cleanly under the narrator line "Sometimes you go back to go forward." (Frame 5) so the VO sits forward, and resolves on a sustained warm chord at Frame 6 so the reel loops seamlessly with no audible seam. Keep it under the diegetic engine, tyre-scream, crystalline-shimmer and wind SFX — poised and present, never a full driving mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

## 🏎️ CONCEPT 04 — PIT LANE FREEZE · The whole crew locks into frozen time — only Aira keeps moving

Pillar / Format: Motorsport / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira — self-aware, fluid, fully human micro-expression; calm pit-lane authority that tips into wonder as time stops

Setting: Floodlit night pit lane, F1-class garage, mid pit-stop chaos frozen into a held breath

Wardrobe (AW): Fitted matte-charcoal fireproof race suit, sponsor-clean (no real logos), sleeves pushed to forearwere — correction: sleeves pushed to mid-forearm, modest high collar, racing gloves tucked at her belt

Hair (AP): Low race-braid, baby hairs slicked, a few flyaways that will later hang frozen mid-air

Makeup (AQ): Matte skin, sweat-sheen on temples, no gloss; eyes clean and sharp

Footwear: Charcoal suede-and-nomex race boots, thin sole, ankle-snug, scuffed toe-box

Accessories: Slim titanium ear-comm (dead/silent), a single thin steel wristband, gloves clipped at hip

The Impossible Move: As Aira steps out of the car, the entire pit crew, the spinning wheel-guns, the flying lug-nuts and the fuel-haze all FREEZE solid in mid-air — and she alone walks through the frozen diorama, weaving between suspended tools and stopped men. (START keyframe = Frame 2 — the instant of freeze; PEAK = Frame 4 — Aira walking through suspended lug-nuts; END keyframe = Frame 6 — time snaps back as she clears the wall.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "Everyone's chasing the clock. She turned it off."

Duration · frames: ~16s · 6 frames

Mood: Held-breath awe, quiet dominance, eerie stillness inside motorsport violence

Composition signature: Frozen kinetic diorama — every other human is a statue, Aira is the only soft-moving thing; depth packed with suspended debris

Camera Language: Descending establishing drone → locked freeze snap → slow dolly-through the frozen crew → FPV weave past a hovering lug-nut → orbit reveal → mirror drone lift on the loop

Grade / Color Script: Sodium-orange floods vs cold cyan garage LEDs; deep teal shadows; warm skin kept true; the freeze moment desaturates the world 12% while Aira stays full-saturated (she is the only living color)

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 5, warm low female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's final pose = next frame's opening pose; debris positions are pinned identically across the frozen frames (2→5) so the join reads as one unbroken time-stop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions across all frames; modest, adult, body-safe; no real sponsor marks

---

### Frame 1 of 6 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

WARDROBE: Matte-charcoal fireproof race suit, high modest collar, sleeves to mid-forearm, gloves clipped at hip, race boots.

HAIR: Low slicked race-braid, sweat-damp baby hairs at temple, two soft flyaways.

MAKEUP: Matte skin, faint temple sweat-sheen, clean sharp eyes, bare lip.

FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES: Charcoal nomex boots scuffed at toe; titanium ear-comm; single steel wristband.

HANDS & NAILS: Bare hands (gloves off, clipped at hip), short clean natural nails, one hand resting on the open car door sill.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: Eyes down-then-lifting, a slow exhale through parted lips, jaw relaxed — a driver collecting herself the instant before chaos.

BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT: Weight rocked onto the leg already out of the cockpit, opposite hand braced on the door, torso uncoiling from the racing seat.

PHYSICS (animated next): Heat-shimmer off brakes, faint fuel-haze drift, floodlight flare bloom — all still LIVE in this frame (pre-freeze).

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): 1) brake heat-haze 2) idling exhaust wisp 3) floodlight bloom 4) suspended dust motes 5) a bead of sweat at her temple 6) loose flyaway hair 7) micro-tremor in braced forearm 8) reflected light crawling on the carbon door.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FG = open car door + her shoulder; MG = Aira rising from cockpit; BG = blurred crew already sprinting in with wheel-guns.

CAMERA ANGLE: High descending establishing DRONE, ~30° down, easing toward her over the car nose.

LIGHTING: KEY = warm sodium flood camera-left; FILL = cool garage LED bounce; RIM = hard cyan edge along braid and shoulder; PRACTICAL = pit-lane light strips; SEPARATION = orange flare behind head; BOUNCE = soft off white pit-wall.

FRAMING: 9:16, Aira lower-third rising into frame, headroom filled with floodlit garage depth.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: An establishing hero-rise framing holds Aira, lower-third rising into frame, headroom filled with floodlit garage depth. She is weight rocked onto the leg already out of the cockpit, opposite hand braced on the door, torso uncoiling from the racing seat. The camera holds a mid distance, Aira the sharp central figure of the Pit Lane Freeze beat, centred in frame. She faces front to the lens. Her pose reads as weight rocked onto the leg already out of the cockpit, opposite hand braced on the door, torso uncoiling from the racing seat — gaze locked dead ahead through the screen, hard and level; head steady, chin level against the G; both shoulders coiled forward over the wheel; the RIGHT hand gripping the wheel with raised tendons; the LEFT hand locked on the opposite rim, knuckles set; torso pinned back into the seat by acceleration, core braced; waist compressed, hips planted in the seat; the RIGHT leg driving the throttle with a flexed ankle while the LEFT braces hard on the floor; both feet committed to the pedals, weight pinned rearward; braid snapping straight back in the rush. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm equivalent, f/2.0, shallow — creamy oval bokeh from floodlights and pit LEDs.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: Cinematic motorsport hero-rise, the quiet before the stop.

ATMOSPHERE: Drifting fuel-haze, hot tarmac shimmer, suspended track dust.

COLOR GRADE: Sodium-orange vs cyan, deep teal shadow, true warm skin.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: Nomex weave, carbon-fiber door weave, sweat micro-droplets, scuffed boot leather.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: Ends on Aira fully standing, hand leaving the door — exact pose Frame 2 opens on.

NEGATIVE: No real logos, no text, no extra fingers, no second face, no gloss makeup, no immodest fit, no warped car geometry.

### Frame 4 of 6 — IMAGE PROMPT (PEAK — FPV through the frozen lug-nuts)

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. TRANSFORM KEYFRAME NOTE: Peak of the impossible move — Aira threads between a constellation of frozen lug-nuts and a suspended fuel-arc, the only living thing in a hanging galaxy of stopped debris.

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 04 (Pit Lane Freeze): A held-breath cinematic score built around a single dramatic time-stop. Opens with a low warm engine-and-PA bed under Frame 1; at the freeze (Frame 2) a deep sub-bass whoom and crystalline shimmer collapse the mix into a cold sustained pad + high ring-tone hum that carries the silent walk (Frames 3–4); the pad bends slowly upward under the FPV weave; ducks fully for the narrator VO in Frame 5 ("Everyone's chasing the clock... she just turned it off."); then a sharp reverse-shimmer and sub-bass thud SLAM the full diegetic chaos back for the restart (Frame 6), resolving on one confident swell into a low warm tail. Tense, awe-struck and commanding, never a full driving mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

---

## 🏙️ CONCEPT 05 — ROOFTOP VELOCITY · Gravity flips mid-vault — she runs the sky like a floor

Pillar / Format: Parkour / SILENT (no VO, no spoken line) · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira — self-aware, fluid, fully human free-runner; total body control, wonder breaking through fierce focus as gravity turns

Setting: Floodlit nighttime city rooftops, neon skyline, a deep gap between two towers, wet ledges glinting, low clouds lit by city glow

Wardrobe (AW): Fitted slate-grey technical free-running suit — stretch panels, taped seams, modest high neckline, sleeves to wrist, nothing loose to snag; a thin charcoal harness-belt (decorative, athletic)

Hair (AP): High tight athletic ponytail, slicked crown, the tail free to whip and later float weightless at the flip

Makeup (AQ): Matte athletic skin, faint exertion-sheen on brow and collarbone, clean sharp eyes, bare lip

Footwear: Low-profile grey-and-black parkour shoes, thin grippy sole, snug laces double-knotted

Accessories: Slim taped wrist-wraps, a thin matte fitness band, a single dark cord at the neck (tucked); nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: At the apex of a rooftop-gap vault, gravity rotates 90° — the far tower's glass wall becomes her new floor and Aira lands running up the side of the building, sprinting across the sky, before gravity snaps back and she lands cleanly on the far roof. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — the vault apex where gravity begins to turn; PEAK = Frame 5 — full wall-run across the vertical glass as if it were ground; END keyframe = Frame 6 — gravity restores, clean roof landing.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: (SILENT — the gravity-turn IS the payoff; no narration, no spoken word.)

Duration · frames: ~28s · 7 frames

Mood: Vertiginous awe, fierce control, weightless wonder

Composition signature: Extreme verticality — deep drops, the horizon line tilting and rotating 90° at the flip so the viewer's sense of up/down turns with her

Camera Language: High establishing drone → low hero push → crane-up vault launch → rotating gravity-turn drone (camera rolls 90° with her) → FPV wall-run → crane landing → mirror drone lift on the loop

Grade / Color Script: Electric neon-noir — cyan-magenta city glow, warm sodium pools, deep ink-blue sky; full saturation, wet specular highlights; at the flip a faint pearl-blue gravity-shimmer washes the rotating world

Voice: SILENT — no narrator VO, no subject speaking; diegetic SFX + master track only

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose = next frame's entry; the horizon's rotation is carried continuously across Frames 3→6 so the 90° turn reads as one unbroken motion; Frame 7 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; modest athletic fit; adult, body-safe; no real signage/logos

---

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 05 (Rooftop Velocity): A vertiginous, weightless cinematic score built around a single gravity-turn. Opens cold and tense under the rooftop perch (Frame 1) with a low sub-pad and a lone high wind-tone; a driving pulse and rhythmic low percussion lock in under the sprint and vault (Frames 2–3); at the gravity-turn (Frames 4–5) the track lifts off into its soaring weightless PEAK — a wide reverberant pad, a pure rising synth lead and a heartbeat sub — timed exactly to the 90° rotation and the wall-run; then it resolves from the peak to a warm triumphant chord on the landing (Frame 6) and settles to a sustained cold chord for the loop (Frame 7). Entirely instrumental, awe-struck and vertiginous, kept under the diegetic wind, foot-strike and shimmer SFX — present, never a full mix, and with no vocals of any kind (silent reel). (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

---

## 🥋 CONCEPT 06 — NEON DOJO · Every strike writes glowing neon-ink calligraphy in the air

Pillar / Format: Martial Arts / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira — self-aware, fluid, fully human martial artist; precise, grounded power with breath-true micro-expressions; serene focus that flares into fierce intent on each strike

Setting: A minimalist dark dojo at night — wet polished-concrete floor mirroring everything, rice-paper screens, a single high shaft of moonlight, cool neon underlighting along the walls, faint incense haze

Wardrobe (AW): Fitted matte-black technical martial gi — wrap-front, modest high neckline, sleeves to mid-forearm, a wide soft obi belt tied clean, full-coverage, nothing loose enough to snag

Hair (AP): High tight warrior-bun, slicked crown, a single clean strand free to react to motion; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Matte athletic skin, faint exertion-sheen on brow and collarbone, clean sharp eyes, bare lip

Footwear: Barefoot — clean, grounded, with taped insteps; feet read true on the wet concrete

Accessories: Thin black hand-wraps across knuckles and wrists, a single dark cord at the neck (tucked); nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: Each strike, block and sweep leaves a glowing trail of liquid neon-ink in the air — punches draw blazing brush-strokes, kicks sweep luminous arcs, and at the peak the accumulated trails resolve into a single perfect glowing calligraphy symbol hanging in the air, which then dissolves into drifting light-motes. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — first strike, first ink-trail; PEAK = Frame 4 — full combo painting the air; the symbol completes at Frame 5; END keyframe = Frame 6 — trails dissolve, she returns to stance.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "Some fight to win. She fights to leave a mark."

Duration · frames: ~22s · 6 frames

Mood: Serene power, ink-on-silk elegance, fierce control

Composition signature: Negative-space minimalism — a lone figure in deep dark, the only color the glowing ink-trails and their mirror in the wet floor

Camera Language: High establishing drone → low hero push → arcing orbit on the first strike → FPV through the painted air → slow rise on the calligraphy reveal → mirror drone lift on the loop

Grade / Color Script: Ink-noir — near-black dojo, cool moon-silver key, neon-cyan and magenta ink-trails, deep teal shadow; full saturation only in the glowing ink against a desaturated room; wet-floor mirror doubling every glow

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 5, warm low female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose = next frame's entry; the ink-trails accumulate continuously across Frames 3→5 (each new trail joins the last) so the painting reads as one unbroken motion; Frame 6 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; modest gi; adult, body-safe; no real logos/script that reads as real-world text (the calligraphy is an abstract glowing glyph)

---

### Frame 2 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Low hero push into the opening form.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Low gimbal/dolly gliding in toward her lead hand; rock-steady.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: Smooth push-in + faint 3° tilt up to her eyes.

LENS: 40mm-equiv, f/2.2.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:03.8–00:04.6` the lead palm extends in a controlled open guard, eyes tracking

`00:04.6–00:05.4` the rear hand chambers hard at the hip, weight loading, the strike priming

PHYSICS & MOTION: Real weight-transfer, authentic pivot water-mist, gi sleeve trailing — grounded.

LIGHTING: Moon-silver key with neon fill; magenta rim travels on the trailing sleeve.

ENVIRONMENT: Dark dojo, neon walls, wet floor reflecting her.

SPEED RAMP: Steady 24fps.

AUDIO (diegetic, timed): low neon hum bed; `00:03.2` a clean barefoot pivot-squeak on wet concrete; `00:03.9` gi-fabric snap as the palm extends; `00:04.8` a sharp exhale (kiai-breath, no word) as the rear hand chambers. Master-track adds a single low rising tone.

DURATION: 6.0s (running total 00:05.4).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the sleeve.

AUDIO (diegetic + VO): low hum bed; `00:09.8` glassy lock-in chime as the glyph completes; NARRATOR VO (AR), warm low female, NOT Aira's mouth — `00:10.6–12.2`: "Some fight to win... she fights to leave a mark." master-track ducks under the VO; `00:12.5` a soft shimmer as motes begin to drift. Aira's mouth stays neutral/closed (no lip-sync).

DURATION: 6.0s (running total 00:13.0).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, light natural blur.

LIP-SYNC: None — VO is external narrator; Aira does not speak.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: Ends on the glyph dissolving, hand lowering — Frame 6 opens on the return to ready-stance.

NEGATIVE: No Aira lip-sync, no real logos/readable text, no extra fingers, no warped anatomy.

### Frame 6 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (END KEYFRAME + LOOP)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Dissolve-to-stillness + mirror drone lift loop-closer.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Cinema drone lifting up-and-back through the moon-shaft, mirroring Frame 1's descent for a seamless loop.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: Rise + slow pull-back as the motes scatter, Aira held centered-lower.

LENS: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:13.0–00:13.6` the glyph fully dissolves into drifting light-motes that scatter into the dark

`00:13.6–00:14.6` Aira lowers her hands and settles back into the opening ready-stance, a slow centering exhale

`00:14.6–00:15.6` the last mote fades; the dojo is still again; the drone lifts and pulls back

`00:15.6–00:16.2` framing matches Frame 1 exactly, ready to loop

PHYSICS & MOTION: Real mote-scatter physics, incense drifting back, natural settle into stance — grounded, matched to Frame 1.

LIGHTING: Moon-silver key; magenta rim; neon walls; floor mirror — matched to Frame 1.

ENVIRONMENT: The still dark dojo restored, empty air.

SPEED RAMP: Steady 24fps.

AUDIO (diegetic + master-track button): `00:13.1` a soft shimmer-fade as the motes scatter; `00:13.8` a gi-fabric rustle as she resets; `00:14.6` her slow centering exhale (no word); the room returns to deep silence + low neon hum (the Frame 1 bed). Master-track resolves on a meditative sustained chord so the reel loops with no audible seam. No words from Aira.

DURATION: 6.0s (running total 00:16.2 active; ~22s with handles).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone rise and motes.

LIP-SYNC: None.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF (LOOP): Final drone elevation/framing matches Frame 1 → seamless loop back to Concept 06 Frame 1.

NEGATIVE: No Aira speaking, no real logos/readable text, no stutter-cut, no warped anatomy, no visible loop seam.

---

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 06 (Neon Dojo): A meditative-to-soaring cinematic score built around ink-on-silk martial elegance. Opens in near-silence under the ready-stance (Frame 1) with a single low drone-pad and a lone shakuhachi-like breath-tone; a soft heartbeat pulse and a sparse plucked motif enter under the opening form (Frame 2); each strike (Frames 3–4) is scored with a crystalline "ink-draw" chime layered over the kiai-breaths, building to a soaring PEAK swell on the spinning kick as the air fills with trails; the track ducks fully for the narrator VO at the glyph reveal (Frame 5 — "Some fight to win... she fights to leave a mark."); then it resolves to a meditative sustained chord as the motes scatter and she returns to stance (Frame 6) for a seamless loop. Elegant, serene and powerful, kept under the diegetic breath, gi-snap and ink-shimmer SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

---

## 🏍️ CONCEPT 07 — LANE SPLIT · Gridlocked traffic dissolves into streams of light-particles as she splits through

Pillar / Format: Moto Hero / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira riding a matte-black naked sportbike, single accent of an electric-blue rim-and-fork glow; self-aware, fluid, fully human rider — calm precision with breath-true micro-expressions, fierce focus behind a clear visor

Setting: A dense city expressway at blue-hour dusk — gridlocked traffic, towering neon billboards, wet tarmac mirroring tail-lights, cool city haze; warm amber tail-lights against a cool blue sky

Wardrobe (AW): A fitted armored motorcycle suit — matte-black with electric-blue seam-piping, high modest collar, full-coverage CE-armored shoulders/elbows/knees, zipped clean, nothing loose to snag

Hair (AP): A sleek low braid tucked into the collar, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free at the visor edge; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a deep berry stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Armored black riding boots with electric-blue lace-flash and reinforced toe, buckles tight, laces tucked

Accessories: A clear-shield matte-black helmet (visor up for identity), armored gloves, a low-profile comms collar (non-speaking); nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: As she lane-splits between the gridlocked cars, each vehicle she passes dissolves into a stream of glowing light-particles — the steel dematerializing into drifting motes that part around her like a curtain, then reform into solid traffic behind her. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — the first car begins dissolving; PEAK = Frame 4 — the whole lane streaming into particles around her; END keyframe = Frame 5 — particles reform to solid traffic behind as she emerges.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "Everyone sees a wall. She sees the gap."

Duration · frames: ~23s · 6 frames

Mood: Cool, surgical, electric-calm

Composition signature: Deep one-point perspective down the traffic corridor, the hero threading a blue-lit needle through warm tail-light walls

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the gridlock → low ground-tracking rig → over-shoulder push into the split → FPV threading the dissolving lane → crane rise on the open-road emerge → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Cool neon-noir — deep blue-hour sky, warm amber tail-lights, electric-blue bike accent, wet-tarmac speculars, deep uncrushed blacks, full saturated mid-tone; the dissolve beat shimmering blue-white particle-light

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 5, warm natural Indian-accented female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the dissolve runs continuously across Frames 3→5 (cars ahead dissolve, cars behind reform) so the split reads as one unbroken motion; Frame 6 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; modest armored suit; adult, body-safe; no real car logos/license text (kept abstract/blurred)

---

### Frame 3 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (START KEYFRAME)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.
