### Frame 3 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT (START KEYFRAME)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Orbit freeze medium; subject + suspending droplets fill ~58%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Orbit gimbal arcing around her, catching the rain freezing as she walks.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: A smooth orbit-arc keeping her walking while the droplets suspend behind and around; one continuous move.

LENS: 35mm-equiv, f/2.2.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:05.6–00:06.0` she strides forward as the rain nearest her begins to freeze and suspend mid-air

`00:06.0–00:06.6` the first droplets stop dead and hang motionless, glittering with neon, the freeze rippling outward

`00:06.6–00:07.4` the suspended field spreads through the alley as she keeps walking, eyes serene

`00:07.4–00:07.8` the field deepens, cueing the full frozen rain

PHYSICS & MOTION: Authentic continuous walk, real rain-freeze with true sudden-suspend (droplets stopping mid-air, not noise), genuine neon-glint — identity locked.

LIGHTING: Cool magenta key; the suspended droplets kick countless tiny neon glints; amber rim.

ENVIRONMENT: The alley with the rain freezing into a suspended field around her.

SPEED RAMP: Ramp 24→96fps across the freeze `00:06.0–06.6` (the suspend blooms in slow-mo), back to 48fps.

AUDIO (diegetic + reaction): rain bed dropping out fast; `00:06.0` a deep sucking time-stop whoomp as the rain freezes + the drumming rain cutting to an eerie crystalline silence + a soft shimmering chime as droplets suspend (cool, awed, musical); a sharp wonder-breath `00:07.0` (no word). Master-track pad begins a slow soaring rise into the silence.

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24→96→48fps, 180° shutter, heavy blur on her walk, the suspended droplets crisp and motionless.

LIP-SYNC: None — breath only.

NEGATIVE: No speaking, no logos, no inventing a new face, no warped limbs, no frame skip, physics violation.

---

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 14 (Alley Standoff): A cool, brooding neon-noir score built around suspense and quiet power. Opens dark and rain-washed under the alley-mouth hook (Frame 1) with a low sub-pulse, a moody minor piano-motif and a distant city-hum; a deliberate noir heartbeat-pulse and a low cello-line enter on the walk (Frame 2); the rain-freeze beats (Frames 3–4) drop the percussion into an eerie crystalline suspension scored with high glassy bead-tones and a soaring string-swell as the storm holds still, building to a transcendent noir PEAK as she walks the frozen rain; the rain-resume (Frame 5) lands a crashing downbeat-stab; the track ducks cleanly under the narrator VO at the resume (Frame 5 — "Even the rain learns to wait."); then it resolves to a sustained moody chord at the alley mouth (Frame 6) for a seamless loop. Cool, brooding and powerful, tempo slow around 76–84 BPM, minor and noir with a glassy neon top. Kept under the diegetic rain, footfall, time-stop and bead-shimmer SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

---

## 🥢 CONCEPT 15 — BLADE FORM · Her blade-trails become glowing light calligraphy hanging in the air

Pillar / Format: Martial Arts / Blade-Form Hero / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira performing a flowing sword kata in a moonlit dojo; self-aware, fluid, fully human martial artist — serene focused mastery with breath-true micro-expressions and precise, weight-true blade-form body-control

Setting: A minimalist dark dojo / ink-wash courtyard at night — paper shoji screens, a single shaft of cold moonlight, drifting incense smoke, a dark polished wood floor, a deep indigo-black space

Wardrobe (AW): A modest charcoal martial training gi with a wrapped obi and wide hakama-style trousers, high modest collar, full-coverage, sleeve-cuffs bound, nothing immodest or loose to snag

Hair (AP): A tight high topknot-bun sealed clean (form-ready), a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a soft rose stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Bare feet or low split-toe tabi with a thin sole, clean and grounded

Accessories: A single elegant straight blade (katana-style, abstract, non-branded), wrapped-handle, a thin wrist-wrap; nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: As she executes each cut of the kata, the blade-trail becomes glowing ink-light calligraphy — each arc and slash leaving a luminous brushstroke hanging in the air like living calligraphy written in light, the strokes blooming into elegant glowing characters around her, then dissolving into drifting embers as she flows to the next cut. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — the first cut leaves the first glowing calligraphy-stroke; PEAK = Frame 4 — the full calligraphy blooms around her; END keyframe = Frame 5 — the calligraphy dissolves as she settles.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "She does not strike. She writes."

Duration · frames: ~22s · 6 frames

Mood: Serene, precise, meditative-powerful

Composition signature: Negative-space minimalism — a lone figure writing arcs of glowing calligraphy in a dark moonlit void

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the dojo → low floor-tracking rig on the form → orbit gimbal at the first cut → FPV through the blooming calligraphy → crane rise at the settle → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Ink-wash moonlight — cold silver-blue moonlight, deep indigo-black, the calligraphy glowing warm gold-white ink-light, deep uncrushed blacks, floor speculars, full saturated mid-tone

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 5, warm natural Indian-accented female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the calligraphy runs continuously across Frames 3→5 (first stroke → full bloom → dissolve) so the kata reads as one unbroken flowing motion; Frame 6 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; modest gi; adult, body-safe; no real logos/readable characters (calligraphy kept abstract glowing strokes)

---

### Frame 1 of 6 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

WARDROBE: The charcoal martial gi with wrapped obi and wide hakama trousers, high modest collar, one cool moonlight-rim sliding along the shoulder, pristine, bound, zero snags.

HAIR: The tight high topknot-bun sealed clean, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free, razor-clean hairline, color locked.

MAKEUP: Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, soft rose stained lip with one wet light-point, inner-corner brighten; real pores.

FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES: Bare feet/split-toe tabi grounded on the dark polished floor; the wrapped-handle blade held in a low ready position; thin wrist-wrap.

HANDS & NAILS: Both hands settled on the blade-handle in a calm ready grip, fingers strong and exact, natural nail-bed color, no white-knuckle tension — disciplined hold.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: Chin level, a calm serene quarter-smile pulling the left corner a hair tighter, lips parted barely 1mm on a held breath, eyes lowered then lifting with a crisp cool moonlight catchlight, the faintest focused set at the outer brow.

BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT: Poised in a low opening stance, torso rotated about 8° to open the shoulder to lens, weight rooted evenly through both feet, knees softly bent, blade low and ready, spine long and centered (living stillness).

PHYSICS (animated next): Incense smoke drifting across the moonlight shaft, a paper screen glowing soft, dust-motes floating in the moonbeam, the free strands stirring in the still air — environment alive, she rooted and poised.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): 1) a free strand stirring at her temple 2) incense smoke drifting across the moonbeam 3) dust-motes floating in the cold light 4) a paper screen glowing soft 5) chest rising on a slow shallow inhale 6) a silver specular sliding along the blade-edge 7) the gi fabric catching a micro rim-shift as she breathes 8) a slow eyelash settle completing a blink.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FG = the blade-tip and her hands nearest lens at ~20% depth, tack sharp; MG = Aira in the opening stance, the hero; BG = the moonlit dojo and shoji screens receding into indigo-black, clean separation, vast minimal negative space.

CAMERA ANGLE: A slow high establishing DRONE easing down over the dojo from above to a clean chest-up hero level — revealing the minimalist moonlit space before settling on her.

LIGHTING: KEY = cold silver-blue moonlight keying from camera-left through the screens; FILL = soft indigo bounce lifting the under-jaw; RIM = a cool moon-edge kissing her shoulder and the blade; PRACTICAL = the moonbeam and a faint screen-glow; SEPARATION = the dojo pulled deep so she reads ~2 stops up; BOUNCE = a polished-floor kick on the gi.

FRAMING: 9:16, Aira centered lower-third, the moonlit void headroom above, the blade in the lower frame, vast negative space to receive the form.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: An establishing-to-hero framing holds Aira, centered lower-third, the moonlit void headroom above, the blade in the lower frame, vast negative space to receive the form. She is poised in a low opening stance, torso rotated about 8° to open the shoulder to lens, weight rooted evenly through both feet, knees softly bent, blade low and ready, spine long and centered. The camera holds a mid distance, Aira the sharp central figure of the Blade Form beat, with the action arcing the upper frame above her. She faces front to the lens. Her pose reads as poised in a low opening stance, torso rotated about 8° to open the shoulder to lens, weight rooted evenly through both feet, knees softly bent, blade low and ready, spine long and centered (living stillness) — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0, shallow — the dojo melting to creamy moonlit bokeh, Aira tack-sharp.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: An ink-wash martial hero still — a lone figure poised with a blade in a moonlit void, scroll-stopping.

ATMOSPHERE: Serene, disciplined, charged — the hush of a held breath before the first cut.

COLOR GRADE: Ink-wash moonlight — cold silver-blue, deep indigo-black, deep uncrushed blacks, floor speculars, full mid-tone, no murk.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: The gi reads as real heavy training cotton, the blade reads true polished steel, the floor reads dark polished wood, soft peach-fuzz catches the rim at her jaw, incense smoke drifts authentically.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: Ends locked on her hands on the blade-handle and the calm lifting gaze — Frame 2 opens on that blade beginning to draw into the first movement of the form.

NEGATIVE: No real logos/readable characters, no extra fingers, no second face, no immodest fit, no warped anatomy, no floating shadows, plastic skin, oversharpened, watermarks.

### Frame 1 of 6 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Establishing-to-hero, chest-up resolve; subject fills ~40% rising to ~55%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Cinema drone descent (high over the dojo → eye level) revealing the moonlit space, settling to a hero frame; one smooth move, no jitter.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: The drone eases down and settles with a faint 4% push-in.

LENS: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:00.0–00:01.2` drone descends over the dojo, the moonlit minimal space revealed, Aira rooted in the opening stance

`00:01.2–00:02.4` she lifts her lowered gaze as the calm serene quarter-smile blooms on a soft inhale

`00:02.4–00:03.2` her grip settles on the blade-handle, the held-breath beat before the first cut

PHYSICS & MOTION: Real drifting incense, genuine floating dust-motes, natural strand stir — no teleporting, no pop-in.

LIGHTING: Cold moonlight key with indigo bounce; cool moon rim steady; moonbeam live; dojo held deep.

ENVIRONMENT: Moonlit dojo at night, shoji screens, incense smoke, polished floor.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time throughout.

AUDIO (diegetic, timed): `00:00` a deep dojo silence — a faint night-insect hush, a distant temple-bell tail and a soft incense-crackle throughout; `00:01.0` a soft gi-rustle as she breathes; `00:01.4` her slow centering exhale (breath only, no words); `00:02.6` a faint blade-ring as her grip settles. Master-track bed barely-there and meditative. No VO this frame.

DURATION: 6.0s (running total 00:03.2).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone move and smoke; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: None — breath only, mouth never forms words.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: Ends on hands on the handle + lifting gaze; Frame 2 begins on the blade drawing into the first movement.

NEGATIVE: No speaking, no logos, no stutter-cut, no warped anatomy, ghosting, audio desync.

### Frame 4 of 6 — IMAGE PROMPT (PEAK — the full calligraphy blooms around her)

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. TRANSFORM KEYFRAME NOTE: This is the PEAK of the impossible move — her flowing kata has written a full bloom of glowing ink-light calligraphy in the air around her, elegant luminous brushstrokes and abstract glowing characters arcing and orbiting where her blade has passed, a living poem of light surrounding the serene figure at its center. 🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 15 (Blade Form): A serene, elegant cinematic score built around meditative power and flow. Opens hushed and spacious under the opening-stance hook (Frame 1) with a lone koto-pluck, a low sub-drone and a distant temple-bell; a slow building shakuhachi-flute line and a soft heartbeat-pulse enter on the form (Frame 2); the calligraphy beats (Frames 3–4) are scored with rising strings, a singing ink-shimmer layer and a swelling koto-and-taiko build as the light-poem blooms, building to a transcendent orchestral PEAK as the full calligraphy surrounds her; the dissolve-settle (Frame 5) lands a warm resolving swell; the track ducks cleanly under the narrator VO at the settle (Frame 5 — "She does not strike. She writes."); then it resolves to a sustained meditative chord at the opening stance (Frame 6) for a seamless loop. Serene, elegant and powerful, tempo broad around 72–80 BPM, modal-Eastern with a warm cinematic top. Kept under the diegetic blade, footfall, ink-shimmer and incense SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

## 🏍️ CONCEPT 16 — CANYON CARVE · Each carve blooms the canyon walls into glowing bioluminescent light · SILENT

Pillar / Format: Moto / Bioluminescent-Bloom Hero / SILENT (music + diegetic only) · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira carving a sport motorcycle through a winding red-rock canyon at dusk; self-aware, fluid, fully human rider — calm fierce mastery with breath-true micro-expressions and precise, weight-true lean-and-carve body-control

Setting: A winding red-rock desert canyon at deep twilight — towering sandstone walls, a ribbon of dark asphalt threading the canyon floor, a deep blue dusk sky bleeding to indigo, first stars, cool shadow pooling in the gorge

Wardrobe (AW): A fitted full-coverage one-piece motorcycle racing suit in charcoal with teal accent-piping and integrated armor, high sealed collar, full-length sleeves and legs, fully modest; a modular helmet worn with the visor up so the face is clearly visible (helmet off and held under the arm in the hero/loop frames)

Hair (AP): A tight low braided bun sealed for the helmet, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free in the hero frames; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a soft berry stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Armored charcoal riding boots with teal lace-flash and a grippy sole, sealed

Accessories: Armored riding gloves, a thin collar-comm (non-speaking), the modular helmet (visor up / held); nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: As she leans and carves each turn, the canyon walls and floor bloom bioluminescent — glowing veins and flowers of cyan-teal-magenta light spreading across the sandstone and the asphalt where her wheels and lean-line pass, a living bloom of light igniting along the carve and trailing behind her like a wake, then softly fading. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — the first carve ignites the first bioluminescent bloom; PEAK = Frame 4 — the full canyon glowing as she carves the deep lean; END keyframe = Frame 5 — the bloom spreads and fades behind as she exits the turn.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: (SILENT — no spoken or narrated line; the blooming bioluminescent canyon IS the payoff.)

Duration · frames: ~26s · 7 frames

Mood: Fierce, serene, wondrous

Composition signature: Sweeping canyon scale — a lone rider carving a ribbon of road that blooms with light through towering dusk walls

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the canyon → low road-tracking rig on the ride → orbit gimbal at the first carve → FPV through the blooming canyon → crane rise at the exit → silent hero-beat eye-macro → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Twilight desert — warm red-rock walls, deep blue-indigo dusk sky, the bioluminescent bloom glowing cyan-teal-magenta, deep uncrushed blacks, asphalt speculars, full saturated mid-tone

Voice: SILENT — no subject voice, no narrator; diegetic SFX + master track only

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the bloom runs continuously across Frames 3→5 (first ignite → full glow → fade-behind) so the ride reads as one unbroken carve; Frame 6 is a silent hero-beat; Frame 7 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; full-coverage racing suit, visor-up so face shows; adult, body-safe; no real logos/readable signage

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Establishing-to-hero, chest-up resolve; subject fills ~40% rising to ~55%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Cinema drone descent (high over the canyon → eye level) revealing the gorge, settling to a hero frame; one smooth move, no jitter.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: The drone eases down and settles with a faint 4% push-in.

LENS: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:00.0–00:01.2` drone descends over the canyon mouth, the dusk gorge revealed, Aira poised beside the bike, helmet under arm

`00:01.2–00:02.4` she lifts her gaze down the canyon as the calm fierce quarter-smile blooms on a soft inhale

`00:02.4–00:03.2` her hand settles on the tank, the held-breath beat before the ride

PHYSICS & MOTION: Real drifting dust, genuine heat-shimmer, natural strand stir — no teleporting, no pop-in.

LIGHTING: Warm last-light key with cool indigo bounce; cool dusk rim steady; dusk sky live; canyon held deep.

ENVIRONMENT: Red-rock canyon at twilight, asphalt ribbon, dusk sky, first stars.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time throughout.

AUDIO (diegetic, timed): `00:00` a wide canyon ambience — a soft desert wind, a distant raptor-cry and a faint settling-tick of cooling asphalt throughout; `00:01.0` a soft suit-creak as she breathes; `00:01.4` her slow centering exhale (breath only, no words); `00:02.6` a faint glove-tap on the tank. Master-track bed barely-there and wondrous. No VO (silent reel).

DURATION: 6.0s (running total 00:03.2).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone move and dust; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: None — breath only, mouth never forms words.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: Ends on hand on the tank + down-canyon gaze; Frame 2 begins on her pulling the helmet on and rolling out.

NEGATIVE: No speaking, no logos, no stutter-cut, no warped anatomy, ghosting, audio desync.

NEGATIVE: No speaking, no real logos, no stutter-cut, no warped anatomy, no visible loop seam, identity flicker.

---

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 16 (Canyon Carve): A fierce, wondrous cinematic score built around motion and awe. Opens wide and spacious under the canyon-mouth hook (Frame 1) with a low desert-drone, a lone duduk-like wind-line and a distant heartbeat-pulse; a driving low-end groove and a tribal-percussion build enter on the ride (Frame 2); the bloom beats (Frames 3–4) layer in rising strings, a shimmering bioluminescent bell-glow and a swelling orchestral-and-synth build as the canyon lights alive, building to a transcendent wondrous PEAK as the full canyon glows; the exit (Frame 5) lands a warm soaring release; the silent hero-beat (Frame 6) drops to a warm sustained pad over the overlook; then it resolves to a sustained wondrous chord at the canyon mouth (Frame 7) for a seamless loop. Fierce, wondrous and powerful, tempo driving around 92–100 BPM, modal-cinematic with a glowing synth-and-orchestral top, and with no vocals of any kind (silent reel). Kept under the diegetic engine, wind, dust and bloom-shimmer SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

---

## 🦅 CONCEPT 17 — WINGSUIT FLOCK · She scatters into a flock of light-birds in flight, then sweeps back and reforms · VO

Pillar / Format: Aerial / Flock-Transform Hero / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira wingsuit-flying through a golden-hour mountain range; self-aware, fluid, fully human flyer — exhilarated serene mastery with breath-true micro-expressions and precise, weight-true wingsuit body-control

Setting: A dramatic mountain range at golden hour — jagged snow-dusted peaks, deep plunging valleys, a glowing sea of clouds below, warm dawn-gold light raking the ridgelines, a vast clean sky bleeding to soft blue above

Wardrobe (AW): A sleek full-coverage aerodynamic wingsuit in charcoal with gold accent-piping and sealed arm-and-leg wings, high sealed collar, fully modest, nothing loose; a streamlined helmet worn with the visor up so the face is clearly visible (helmet on, visor up, throughout flight; helmet off and held in the hero/loop cliff frames)

Hair (AP): A tight low braided bun sealed for the helmet, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free in the hero frames; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a soft coral stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Streamlined charcoal flight-boots with gold lace-flash and the wingsuit foot-wing, sealed

Accessories: Aerodynamic flight gloves, an altimeter wrist-piece, a thin collar-comm (non-speaking), the helmet; nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: As she flies, she scatters into a flock of glowing light-birds — her form dissolving at the trailing edges into a wheeling flock of luminous gold-white birds that scatter and bank across the sky, then the flock sweeps back together and reforms seamlessly into Aira flying, whole and exhilarated. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — she begins to scatter into the first birds; PEAK = Frame 4 — the full flock wheels across the sky; END keyframe = Frame 5 — the flock sweeps back and reforms into Aira.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "To soar, she let herself scatter."

Duration · frames: ~26s · 7 frames

Mood: Exhilarated, serene, transcendent

Composition signature: Vast aerial scale — a lone flyer scattering into a wheeling flock of light against a golden sea of peaks

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the peaks → tracking dive-rig on the launch → orbit gimbal at the scatter → FPV through the wheeling flock → crane/aerial sweep at the reform → hero VO beat → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Golden-hour alpine — warm dawn-gold ridgelines, glowing cloud-sea, soft blue sky, the flock glowing gold-white light, deep uncrushed shadow, full saturated mid-tone

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 6, warm natural Indian-accented female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the flock runs continuously across Frames 3→5 (scatter → full flock → reform) so the flight reads as one unbroken glide; Frame 7 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; full-coverage wingsuit, visor-up so face shows; adult, body-safe; no real logos/readable signage

---

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

WARDROBE: The charcoal aerodynamic wingsuit with gold piping and sealed wings, high sealed collar, one warm dawn-rim sliding along the shoulder, pristine, sealed, zero snags.

HAIR: The tight low braided bun sealed clean, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free, razor-clean hairline, color locked.

MAKEUP: Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, soft coral stained lip with one wet light-point, inner-corner brighten; real pores.

FOOTWEAR & ACCESSORIES: Streamlined flight-boots with gold lace-flash planted at the cliff-edge; flight gloves; the helmet held under one arm; altimeter wrist-piece; collar-comm.

HANDS & NAILS: One gloved hand resting at the wingsuit hip, the other cradling the helmet, fingers strong and easy, natural nail-bed color, no tension — poised confidence.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: Chin level, a calm exhilarated quarter-smile pulling the left corner a hair tighter, lips parted barely 1mm on a held breath, eyes direct out over the peaks with a crisp twin dawn catchlight, the faintest focused set at the outer brow.

BIOMECHANICS & WEIGHT: Poised at the cliff-edge exit, torso rotated about 8° to open the shoulder to lens, weight settled on the back leg with a 3° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long and ready (living stillness).

PHYSICS (animated next): High alpine wind stirring fine snow-spindrift off the ridge, cloud-sea drifting far below, dawn-light pulsing on the peaks, the free strands stirring — environment alive, she poised.

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): 1) a free strand stirring at her temple 2) fine snow-spindrift lifting off the ridge 3) the cloud-sea drifting far below 4) dawn-light pulsing on a far peak 5) chest rising on a slow shallow inhale 6) a gold specular sliding the wingsuit piping 7) the suit catching a micro rim-shift as she breathes 8) a slow eyelash settle completing a blink.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FG = the rocky cliff-edge and her boot nearest lens at ~20% depth, tack sharp; MG = Aira at the exit, the hero; BG = the vast mountain range and glowing cloud-sea falling away, clean separation, immense scale.

CAMERA ANGLE: A slow high establishing DRONE easing down over the ridge from above to a clean chest-up hero level — revealing the mountain scale before settling on her.

LIGHTING: KEY = warm dawn-gold raking from camera-left across the peaks; FILL = soft cool sky-bounce lifting the under-jaw; RIM = a warm dawn-edge kissing her shoulder and the helmet; PRACTICAL = the rising sun and glowing cloud-sea; SEPARATION = the range pulled deep so she reads ~2 stops up; BOUNCE = a snow-kick on the suit.

FRAMING: 9:16, Aira centered lower-third, the peaks and sky headroom above, the cliff in the lower frame, vast negative space over the valley.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: An establishing-to-hero framing holds Aira, centered lower-third, the peaks and sky headroom above, the cliff in the lower frame, vast negative space over the valley. She is poised at the cliff-edge exit, torso rotated about 8° to open the shoulder to lens, weight settled on the back leg with a 3° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long and ready. The camera holds a mid distance, Aira the sharp central figure of the Wingsuit Flock beat, with the action arcing the upper frame above her. She faces front to the lens. Her pose reads as poised at the cliff-edge exit, torso rotated about 8° to open the shoulder to lens, weight settled on the back leg with a 3° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, spine long and ready (living stillness) — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0, shallow — the range melting to creamy golden bokeh, Aira tack-sharp.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: A golden-hour aerial hero still — a lone flyer poised at a cliff-edge above a sea of peaks, scroll-stopping.

ATMOSPHERE: Exhilarated, serene, charged — the hush of a held breath before the leap.

COLOR GRADE: Golden-hour alpine — warm gold ridgelines, glowing cloud-sea, soft blue sky, deep uncrushed shadow, full mid-tone, no murk.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: The wingsuit reads as real technical aero-fabric, the rock reads true weathered granite, the snow reads true crystalline spindrift, soft peach-fuzz catches the rim at her jaw, spindrift lifts authentically.

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF: Ends locked on her hand at the hip and the calm out-over-the-peaks gaze — Frame 2 opens on her pulling the helmet on and diving off the edge into flight.

NEGATIVE: No real logos/readable signage, no extra fingers, no second face, no immodest fit, no warped anatomy, no floating shadows, plastic skin, oversharpened, watermarks.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Establishing-to-hero, chest-up resolve; subject fills ~40% rising to ~55%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Cinema drone descent (high over the ridge → eye level) revealing the range, settling to a hero frame; one smooth move, no jitter.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: The drone eases down and settles with a faint 4% push-in.

LENS: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:02.4–00:03.2` her hand settles at the wingsuit hip, the held-breath beat before the leap

LIGHTING: Warm dawn key with cool sky bounce; warm dawn rim steady; sun live; range held deep.

ENVIRONMENT: Golden-hour mountain range, cliff-edge, cloud-sea, peaks.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time throughout.

AUDIO (diegetic, timed): `00:00` a high alpine ambience — a thin cold wind, a distant eagle-cry and a faint ridge-hush throughout; `00:01.0` a soft suit-flutter as she breathes; `00:01.4` her slow centering exhale (breath only, no words); `00:02.6` a faint glove-flex at the hip. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0, shallow — matched to Frame 1.

NEGATIVE: No real logos/readable signage, no extra fingers, no second face, no immodest fit, no warped anatomy, no visible loop seam.

### Frame 7 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT (LOOP)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

`00:20.0–00:21.0` the framing matches Frame 1 exactly, ready to loop

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 17 (Wingsuit Flock): A soaring, transcendent cinematic score built around freedom and awe. Opens vast and airy under the cliff-edge hook (Frame 1) with a high sustained string-pad, a lone wordless-soprano-like wind-line and a distant heartbeat-pulse; a lifting orchestral swell and a soft pulse enter on the dive (Frame 2); the flock beats (Frames 3–4) layer in a shimmering choir-glow, fluttering harp-and-string runs and a swelling orchestral-and-synth build as she scatters into birds of light, building to a transcendent soaring PEAK as the full murmuration wheels; the reform (Frame 5) lands a warm resolving swell; the track ducks cleanly under the narrator VO at the line beat (Frame 6 — "To soar, she let herself scatter."); then it resolves to a sustained transcendent chord at the cliff-edge (Frame 7) for a seamless loop. Soaring, free and powerful, tempo broad around 76–84 BPM, major-lifted orchestral with a glowing choir-and-synth top. Kept under the diegetic wind, wing-flutter and light-wingbeat SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

## 🌪️ CONCEPT 18 — DESERT DRIFT STORM · Her drift summons a towering sand-vortex she carves around and rides out · VO

Pillar / Format: Kinetic Hero / Storm-Summon / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira sliding a stripped rally buggy across an open desert at golden hour; self-aware, fluid, fully human driver — fierce serene mastery with breath-true micro-expressions and precise, weight-true drift body-control

Setting: A vast open desert at golden hour — rolling amber dunes, drifting heat-dust, a huge bleeding-gold sky, a low blazing sun, long blue shadows pooling between dunes, a sea of sand to the horizon

Wardrobe (AW): A fitted full-coverage charcoal rally racing suit with gold accent-piping and integrated harness, high sealed collar, full-length sleeves and legs, fully modest; a modular helmet worn with the visor up so the face is clearly visible (helmet off and held under the arm in the hero/loop frames)

Hair (AP): A tight low braided bun sealed for the helmet, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free in the hero frames; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — warm satin skin, soft bronze contour, defined brow, a soft terracotta stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Armored charcoal rally boots with gold lace-flash and a grippy sole, sealed

Accessories: Armored rally gloves, a thin collar-comm (non-speaking), the modular helmet (visor up / held); nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: As she throws the buggy into a wide drift, the swirling sand spins up into a towering sand-vortex — a colossal gold-lit tornado of sand and light rising off her drift-arc, and she carves around and through the base of the vortex she has summoned, then rides out clean as it collapses back to drifting dust behind her. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — the drift begins spinning the sand up into the first vortex; PEAK = Frame 4 — the full towering sand-vortex as she carves around it; END keyframe = Frame 5 — the vortex collapses behind as she rides out.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "She doesn't outrun the storm — she summons it."

Duration · frames: ~24s · 6 frames

Mood: Fierce, primal, awe-struck

Composition signature: Vast desert scale — a lone buggy carving a drift-arc that spins a towering vortex of gold sand into the huge sky

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the dunes → low sand-tracking chase rig on the drift → orbit gimbal at the spin-up → FPV through the vortex base → crane rise at the ride-out → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Golden-hour desert — warm amber dunes, bleeding-gold sky, the vortex glowing gold-white with sun-shot sand, deep uncrushed blue shadow, full saturated mid-tone

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 5, warm natural Indian-accented female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the vortex runs continuously across Frames 3→5 (spin-up → full tower → collapse) so the drift reads as one unbroken slide; Frame 6 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; full-coverage rally suit, visor-up so face shows; adult, body-safe; no real logos/readable signage

---

Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Loop-close establishing; subject ~45% as the drone climbs.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Cinema drone pull-up (hero level → high over the dunes), the mirror of Frame 1's descent.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: The drone rises and pulls back to the Frame 1 establishing position; one smooth move, no jitter.

LENS: 35mm-equiv, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:13.8–00:14.8` she resets beside the buggy at the desert's edge, hand on the roll-cage, helmet under arm, the composed fierce quarter-smile returning

`00:14.8–00:16.0` a held breath as the drone climbs over the dunes

`00:16.0–00:17.0` the framing matches Frame 1 exactly, ready to loop

PHYSICS & MOTION: Real drifting heat-dust and spindrift shifting with the drone parallax, natural strand stir — matched to Frame 1.

LIGHTING: Warm sun key, cool sky bounce, warm sun rim — matched to Frame 1.

ENVIRONMENT: The golden-hour desert, dunes, heat-dust, gold sky.

SPEED RAMP: 100% real-time.

Master-track resolves on a sustained primal chord so the reel loops with no audible seam. No words from Aira.

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180° shutter, natural blur on the drone climb; real-time.

LIP-SYNC: None — lips composed.

NEGATIVE: No Aira speaking, no real logos, no stutter-cut, no warped anatomy, no visible loop seam, identity flicker.

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 18 (Desert Drift Storm): A fierce, primal cinematic score built around power and awe. Opens wide and arid under the desert-edge hook (Frame 1) with a low desert-drone, a lone duduk-and-bone-flute line and a distant heartbeat-pulse; a driving low-end groove and a heavy tribal-percussion build enter on the drift (Frame 2); the vortex beats (Frames 3–4) layer in thunderous taiko, a rising war-string surge and a swelling orchestral-and-synth build as the storm towers, building to a primal awe-struck PEAK as she carves the colossal vortex; the ride-out (Frame 5) lands a triumphant soaring release; the track ducks cleanly under the narrator VO at the ride-out (Frame 5 — "She doesn't outrun the storm — she summons it."); then it resolves to a sustained primal chord at the desert edge (Frame 6) for a seamless loop. Fierce, primal and powerful, tempo driving around 96–104 BPM, modal-cinematic with a thunderous percussion-and-orchestral top. Kept under the diegetic engine, wind, sand-roar and vortex SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

## 🌃 CONCEPT 19 — MAG-LEV ROOFTOP RUN · Neon light-trails wrap her and she mag-lev lifts, gliding weightless across the rooftop gap · VO

Pillar / Format: Kinetic Hero / Mag-Lev Levitation / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira sprinting and vaulting across neon megacity rooftops at night; self-aware, fluid, fully human free-runner — fierce serene mastery with breath-true micro-expressions and precise, weight-true parkour body-control

Setting: A neon megacity skyline at night — a forest of glowing towers, holographic signage washing magenta-cyan-gold, a deep glittering chasm of streets far below, wet rooftop concrete mirroring the neon, low drifting city-haze

Wardrobe (AW): A fitted full-coverage charcoal athletic tech-runner suit with reactive gold-and-cyan accent-lines, a high sealed collar, long sleeves and legs, a light sealed harness, fully modest, nothing loose; soft-shell, aerodynamic

Hair (AP): A tight high braided bun with a clean center part and lacquered control, a few face-framing strands free to react to motion; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a deep plum stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Sleek charcoal free-running shoes with a grippy sole and reactive cyan lace-flash, laces tucked

Accessories: Fitted tech gloves, a slim wrist-console (non-speaking), a thin collar-comm; nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: As she sprints and launches off a rooftop edge into a chasm-gap, glowing neon light-trails wrap around her body and she mag-lev lifts — ribbons of magenta-cyan-gold light spiralling around her as gravity releases its hold, and she glides weightless across the chasm on a wrap of neon light, then the light unspools and sets her down in a clean run on the far rooftop. (START keyframe = Frame 3 — the light-trails begin wrapping as she launches; PEAK = Frame 4 — the full mag-lev weightless glide across the gap wrapped in neon; END keyframe = Frame 5 — the light unspools and she lands in a run on the far rooftop.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "Gravity was only ever a suggestion."

Duration · frames: ~24s · 6 frames

Mood: Fierce, weightless, transcendent

Composition signature: Vertiginous city scale — a lone runner wrapped in neon light gliding weightless across a glowing chasm of towers

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the rooftops → low tracking rig on the sprint → orbit gimbal at the light-wrap launch → FPV through the mag-lev glide over the chasm → crane rise at the landing → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Neon-noir night — magenta-cyan-gold holographic wash, wet-concrete speculars, the light-wrap glowing magenta-cyan-gold, deep uncrushed blacks, full saturated mid-tone

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 5, warm natural Indian-accented female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the light-wrap runs continuously across Frames 3→5 (wrap → full glide → unspool-land) so the run reads as one unbroken motion; Frame 6 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; full-coverage tech-runner suit; adult, body-safe; no real logos/readable signage

FRAME-JOIN HANDOFF (LOOP): Resolves to the exact Frame 1 hook (hand on parapet, out-over-skyline gaze, drone high) so Frame 6 → Frame 1 loops invisibly.

NEGATIVE: No Aira speaking, no real logos, no stutter-cut, no warped anatomy, no visible loop seam, identity flicker.

---

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 19 (Mag-Lev Rooftop Run): A fierce, weightless neon-noir cinematic score built around freedom and momentum. Opens cool and airy under the rooftop-edge hook (Frame 1) with a high synth-pad, a lone glassy arpeggio and a distant sub-pulse; a driving pulse and a tight propulsive groove enter on the sprint (Frame 2); the mag-lev beats (Frames 3–4) layer in a shimmering glass-choir, rising synth-arpeggio runs and a swelling synth-and-strings build as the light wraps her and she lifts weightless, building to a transcendent soaring PEAK as she glides the chasm; the landing (Frame 5) lands a triumphant resolving swell; the track ducks cleanly under the narrator VO at the landing (Frame 5 — "Gravity was only ever a suggestion."); then it resolves to a sustained electric chord at the parapet (Frame 6) for a seamless loop. Fierce, weightless and powerful, tempo driving around 100–108 BPM, minor-lifted synth-orchestral with a glowing glass-and-arp top. Kept under the diegetic wind, foot-strike, mag-lev-hum and city SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)

---

## 🏁 CONCEPT 20 — ESCAPE VELOCITY (FINALE) · She launches the hypercar into orbit and breaks to starlight, then reforms among the stars · VO

Pillar / Format: Kinetic Hero / Finale / Orbital-Break Transform / VO · 9:16 vertical reel · VEO 3.1 (motion) + Nano Banana Pro (stills)

Hero: Aira at the wheel of a matte-silver hypercar launching across a night salt-flat into orbit; self-aware, fluid, fully human driver — transcendent serene mastery with breath-true micro-expressions and precise, weight-true body-control

Setting: A vast night salt-flat launch strip under an immense star-field — cracked white salt mirroring the stars, a low silver moon, a thin ground-mist, the Milky Way arcing overhead; transitioning through the upper atmosphere to the black of space over the glowing blue-green curve of Earth

Wardrobe (AW): A fitted full-coverage charcoal-and-silver racing suit with cyan accent-piping and integrated harness, high sealed collar, full-length sleeves and legs, fully modest; a streamlined helmet worn with the visor up so the face is clearly visible (helmet off and held under the arm in the hero/loop frames)

Hair (AP): A tight low braided bun sealed for the helmet, a clean center part, a few face-framing strands free in the hero frames; color locked

Makeup (AQ): Clean kinetic glow — neutral satin skin, soft contour, defined brow, a soft rose stained lip, inner-corner brighten; real pores

Footwear: Streamlined charcoal driving boots with cyan lace-flash and a grippy sole, sealed

Accessories: Aerodynamic driving gloves, a slim wrist-console (non-speaking), a thin collar-comm, the helmet; nothing dangling

The Impossible Move: She launches the hypercar at escape velocity, the car lifts off the salt-flat and climbs into orbit, the world falling away below — then at the apex over the curve of Earth she and the car break apart into a streaming constellation of starlight, a galaxy of light scattering across space, before the starlight sweeps back and reforms into Aira soaring whole among the stars. (START keyframe = Frame 4 — the car lifts off and breaks free of gravity; PEAK = Frame 6 — she and the car break apart into starlight at the apex; END keyframe = Frame 7 — the starlight reforms into Aira soaring among the stars.)

Concept Line / Twist payoff: "The sky was never the limit — it was only ever the start line."

Duration · frames: ~32s · 8 frames

Mood: Transcendent, triumphant, awe-struck — the grand finale

Composition signature: Earth-to-orbit scale — a lone car launching off a star-mirrored salt-flat, climbing into space, breaking to a galaxy of light over the curve of Earth

Camera Language: High establishing drone over the salt-flat → in-car gimbal at ignition → low ground-tracking rig on the launch → orbit gimbal at lift-off → FPV through the orbital climb → wide cosmic break to starlight → crane/aerial sweep at the reform → mirror drone pull-up to loop

Grade / Color Script: Night-to-cosmos — silver moonlit salt-flat, deep star-field, the orbital climb bleeding to atmospheric blue then black space, the starlight-break glowing gold-white-cyan over the blue-green curve of Earth, deep uncrushed blacks, full saturated mid-tone

Narrator VO (AR): One line, landed near Frame 7, warm natural Indian-accented female narrator (NOT Aira's mouth, never subject-speaking)

Frame-Join Continuity: Each frame's exit pose/cue = next frame's entry; the transform runs continuously across Frames 4→7 (lift-off → climb → break to starlight → reform) so the launch reads as one unbroken ascent; Frame 8 mirrors Frame 1 for a seamless loop

Identity safety: Same Aira face/proportions every frame; full-coverage racing suit, visor-up so face shows; adult, body-safe; no real logos/readable signage

---

### Frame 7 of 8 — VIDEO PROMPT (VO lands here)

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Crane/aerial cosmic sweep medium-close; subject fills ~55%.

DRONE / RIG NOTE: Virtual aerial/crane cosmic sweep (arc to hero level); smooth, transcendent.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: The sweep arcs and settles with a faint 4% push, one elevating move.

LENS: 50mm-equiv, f/2.0.

SUBJECT ACTION — MICRO-TIMECODE:

`00:16.0–00:17.2` the scattered starlight sweeps back and reforms into Aira soaring whole and weightless among the stars over the curve of Earth

`00:17.2–00:18.4` her triumphant transcendent half-smile breaks as she exhales release, the last sparks fading

`00:18.4–00:19.6` she holds the alive, soaring look over the glowing curve of Earth

PHYSICS & MOTION: Real reforming-starlight settle into her whole form, authentic weightless soar, natural smile bloom — grounded gesture-physics; identity exactly restored.

LIGHTING: Earth-shine key, clean deep-space fill, steady fading starlight rim.

ENVIRONMENT: Deep space over the curve of Earth, Aira soaring whole.

SPEED RAMP: 48fps easing to 100% real-time.

AUDIO (diegetic + VO): cosmic hush; `00:16.1` a soft settling starlight-chime as she reforms + a deep warm celestial drone; NARRATOR VO (AR), warm natural Indian-accented female, NOT Aira's mouth — `00:16.8–18.8`: "The sky was never the limit — it was only ever the start line." master-track ducks under the VO; a release exhale `00:18.4` (breath only). ---

🎚️ MASTER TRACK BRIEF — Concept 20 (Escape Velocity · Finale): The grand-finale score — a transcendent, triumphant cinematic anthem built around awe and release. Opens vast and hushed under the salt-flat hook (Frame 1) with a deep cosmic sub-drone, a lone celestial-choir note and a distant heartbeat-pulse; a low pulsing build and a tense rising bed enter on the ignition and launch (Frames 2–3); the lift-off and orbital climb (Frames 4–5) layer in thunderous taiko, a soaring war-string surge and a swelling full-orchestra-and-synth build as the car breaks free of gravity and climbs into space; the starlight-break (Frame 6) hits the immense magical PEAK — a soaring full orchestral-and-choir swell landed on the constellation of light; the reform (Frame 7) lands the triumphant, tear-in-the-eye resolve; the track ducks cleanly under the narrator VO at the reform (Frame 7 — "The sky was never the limit — it was only ever the start line."); then it resolves to a sustained transcendent chord at the salt-flat (Frame 8) for a seamless loop that also closes the entire 20-concept set. Transcendent, triumphant and immense, tempo building around 80–92 BPM, epic-cinematic with a full orchestral-choir-and-synth top. Kept under the diegetic drive, thrust, climb-roar and starlight SFX — present, never a full mix. (Distinct from every other Master Track Brief in this set.)
